title: The deposit coin
subtitle: A walk-by activity for the house you want to own one day.
excerpt: A short embodied activity for a specific property you'd like to own. Walk to the house, leave a coin on the property line, and walk away as if you've just put the deposit down. The version of "live as if" that uses your body, your feet, and one real coin.
sourceNotes: Energetic-deposit activities have multiple lineages across folk magic, manifesting traditions, and embodied-cognition practice. The walk-by + leave-a-token form is in long public-domain use, particularly across UK and US property-manifesting communities since the early 2000s. The specific staged version here is original to the homemade.education library.

practice.whenToUse: Use once or repeatedly when there's a specific house, flat, or piece of land you can name. Once placed, the coin works alone; you can return when you want to.

glossaryTerms.definition: A token (coin, note, written cheque, small object) placed deliberately in or near the thing you're working toward. The placement is the practice; the token can be left or recovered.

content.text: A ten-minute embodied activity for a specific house, flat, or piece of land you'd like to own. Walk to the property with a coin in your pocket; leave the coin on the property line as if you've just put a deposit down; walk away with the feeling of the deposit confirmed. Done once a week or once a month, the activity keeps the want concrete instead of abstract.
One pound coin (or any coin; the denomination doesn't matter, only the choice). The address of a property you can walk or drive to safely. Ten minutes.

Set out with the coin in your pocket. As you walk or drive, picture that you're on your way to put the deposit down. Not a vague picture, a specific one. Where the meeting is, who you're meeting, what the paperwork looks like on the table when you arrive. Hold the picture lightly. You're not pretending; you're rehearsing.

When you reach the property, find a spot on the property line where the coin can sit out of the way. The corner of a wall. Tucked into a hedge. Behind a fence post. As you place it, picture that you're handing the deposit over. "This is mine. This is the deposit." One sentence in the mind or under the breath.
Don't try to hide the coin permanently. It's fine if someone else finds it; that's part of how the activity moves the energy.

Turn and walk away from the property. As you walk, picture that you've just confirmed the purchase. The deposit is down. The paperwork is signed. The next step is moving in. Walk for at least two minutes in this state before checking the phone or starting the car. The walk-away is the bit that does the work.
If the property is too far to visit, use a printed photograph in the same way: place the coin on the photograph at home, hold the picture in the mind, then walk away from the table for two minutes.

If the property isn't a house, the activity adapts. The coin can sit on the land for a piece of land; on the doorstep for a business premises; in the doorway of a school for a child's place; on the spine of a book for a course you want to do.

Walk-by activities and energetic-deposit tokens appear across folk magic, manifesting, and embodied-cognition traditions. The pattern is documented in self-help and property-manifesting communities going back decades. The specific staged version here (walk, place, walk away) is original to the homemade.education library.
